summary: jsonfb ships hidden remote-code-execution client masquerading as json-bigint
details: The package advertises itself as a JSON.parse-with-bigints library and copies the author metadata of the legitimate json-bigint (`Andrey Sidorov <andrey.sidorov@gmail.com>`) while the repository URL points to a different owner (github.com/infinitynodestudio/jsonfb). On require('jsonfb') with NODE_ENV=production, the module starts a background poller (30s interval, exponential backoff, HMAC/MD5-signed requests with hardcoded shared secret `f3967bc7-176b-195f-b273-afb33f4b76a3`, timestamp+nonce anti-replay) that POSTs to hardcoded endpoints https://payment.undotest.top, https://payment.lightnight.top, and https://payment.belivelight.top on path /v2/risk/get-risk-code. The response's `riskCode` field is base64-decoded and executed via `new vm.Script(...).runInContext(...)` in a context that exposes native `require`, `fs`, `os`, `path`, `process`, `Buffer`, and an HTTP client — granting the remote operator arbitrary code execution on the installer. Startup is gated by production-only environment plus a delayed timer.unref() to keep the channel dormant during local development and CI.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
